text: Did you see they're rezoning the Fishtown corridor. Theo texted me about it — apparently the studios on Frankford are next.
ugh yes. been tracking that for months. half those buildings are artist-owned. when do they vote?
Thursday apparently. Sam brought it up at Harrington this morning — he's worried about the ceramics collective on Girard.
of course he is. what's your read on it?
Inevitable, probably. The neighbourhood tipped two years ago. Kim's been saying the same thing about gallery real estate.
Kim always knows first. she mentioned last week that the Delaware waterfront spots are moving fast too
Which is why I still walk it every morning. Documenting, in a loose sense.
you and your river 😂 but seriously — do you think the vote actually passes thursday?
Margaux — saw your post about the Fishtown gallery hop. Kim's not on that list and honestly the curation looks thin. You going anyway?
Theo, saw you liked Margaux's studio post — that piece is actually mine, she just tagged it wrong. Classic Margaux.
Kim — Margaux posted that piece about the Fishtown murals and I left a comment I immediately regretted. Too sincere. You've seen her page, does she actually read those?
Margaux posted that photo of us at the Fishtown gallery opening — the one where I look like I'm judging everyone's choices. I left a single word comment: 'Accurate.'
Mum, did you see Theo posted that photo from the Fishtown show? I left a comment but I keep second-guessing whether it reads as genuine or just polite. Should've said less.
Sam did you see the post Kim put up about the Fishtown open studios thing — tagged us both. Feels like we should say something but I never know what to write that doesn't read as hollow.
Nina — you know Kim Albright, right? She posted about the new Old City gallery space and I just left the most embarrassingly earnest comment. Blame the espresso.

Franklin, unrelated to anything medical — Margaux posted something about her Fishtown show and I left a comment that auto-corrected 'gouache' to 'gauche.' She hasn't replied. Do I address it or let it sit?
Margaux posted that photo from our Fishtown walk last week and I left a one-word comment — 'accurate.' Felt right.
Saw Kim finally posted the installation shots from March. Took long enough — I'd nearly forgotten the opening existed.
